articles.title: Dollar Advances as Iran War Rages
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) rose to a 1-week high on Friday and finished up by +0.27%. The dollar moved higher on Friday as the risk of a protracted Iran war boosts safe-haven demand for the dollar. Also, Friday's +5% rally in crude oil prices is pushing inflation expectations higher,...

articles.title: Stocks Fall and Bond Yields Rise on Concern About a Protracted Iran War

articles.title: Borrowed peace pays hefty interest – North American Session Market Wrap for March 27
articles.expand: March 27th, 2026 North-American Session Recap – After a hopeful week, the theme is souring again, as Traders prepare for a heavy week and an even heavier weekend. Check up the latest trends and developments, daily asset and FX performance, what changed fundamentally and what's coming up in the session ahead.

articles.title: Major US stock indices close lower. Major indices close lower for the 5th week.
articles.expand: The major US stock indices are closing lower for the day and lower for the week. The decline this week is the 5th consecutive weekly decline. The S P decline is the worst string in 4 years. A look at the closing levels shows: Dow industrial average-793.78 or -1.73% at 45166.33. S P index fell -108.49 or -1.67% at 6368.67. NASDAQ index fell -459.72 ...

articles.title: CNY: PBoC seen resisting strong appreciation – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank economists Dr. Henry Hao and Volkmar Baur say China’s industrial profits surged early in 2026, led by AI-related electronics, but this strength predates the recent energy shock.

articles.title: Houthis in Yemen announce entry into the conflict if any alliance joins the US and Israel
articles.expand: This day just keeps getting worse. At the start of trading Monday it looked like Trump was on track to get some kind of ceasefire by now. Instead, we have talk of bombing steel plants and now this. The Houthis in Yemen say they will enter the conflict on the side of Iran if any alliance joins the US and Israel. The possible chokepoint now is the th...

articles.title: Nasdaq falls below 21,000 for the first time since August. TSLA shares down more than 3%
articles.expand: The mood in markets continues to deteriorate into the weekend. I don't know if that's pricing in the chance of a weekend escalation or it's a realization the war could go on for another month (or longer) and that could cripple energy markets. Tech had been vulnerable to AI narratives unwinding all year and it's worsening now. I worry that we're sti...

articles.title: Markets Weekly Outlook - Middle East uncertainty to dominate ahead of jobs report, Nasdaq 100 at 6-month lows
articles.expand: The Weekly Outlook details how Middle East uncertainty and oil supply crises drove the Nasdaq into correction territory and pushed the VIX to fever pitch. It examines the dollar's surge and key movements in oil and gold. Plus, a look ahead at the crucial US Jobs Report, Eurozone inflation data, and central bank pressures.

articles.title: Stocks reach new lows as War goes on – Dow Jones and US Stock Market Outlook
articles.expand: S P 500, Dow Jones, Nasdaq Analysis and Trading Levels: Equities face intense risk-aversion as the Nasdaq and S P 500 hit new conflict lows, dropping over 1% on the session. Surging Brent crude above $110 reawakens inflation fears while the impending weekend arrival of a 4,500-strong Marine fleet escalates geopolitical anxiety. Explore an intraday ...

articles.title: ECB Schnabel: There is no need to rush into action
articles.expand: ECB's Schnabel is speaking and says: We are facing a massive energy price shock. There is no need to rush into action. We have time to analyze the data. ECB should not be rushed to raise rates. This has a stagflation-lite feel—the energy shock keeps inflation risks alive, but the wait-and-see stance tells you the ECB isn’t in a hurry to support gro...

articles.title: USDJPY cracks above 160.00 for the first time since July 2024
articles.expand: The USDJPY has pushed decisively above the 160.00 level, extending to a high of 160.29. In doing so, the pair briefly moved above a key swing area from 2024 near 160.25, but the break lacked follow-through, with the move only clearing that zone by a handful of pips before stalling. That hesitation at a prior multi-year reference point is worth noti...

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: War headlines and US employment to set the tone
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair ends the week around 1.1530, barely below its opening but off the peak at 1.1640. Widespread optimism on Monday was diluted as days went by, and financial markets moved once again on war-related headlines.

articles.title: ECB: Oil shock complicates path – ING
articles.expand: ING notes that ECB officials have offered little resistance to hawkish market pricing as Oil rises, reinforcing front-end EUR rate expectations.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP remains vulnerable amid Mideast crisis
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) showed some resilience against the US Dollar (USD), holding gains from the previous week’s recovery, when the Bank of England (BoE) opted for a hawkish hold.

articles.title: Technology takes a hit, energy and consumer staples show strength
articles.expand: Technology takes a hit, energy and consumer staples show strength The US stock market today presented a dynamic landscape, characterized by significant shifts across various sectors. Notably, the technology sector faced headwinds, while energy and consumer staples showed resilience in the turbulent market conditions. 📉 Technology Sector: Facing Cha...

articles.title: GBP/USD Weekly Forecast: Pound Sterling remains vulnerable amid Mideast crisis
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) showed some resilience against the US Dollar (USD), holding gains from the previous week’s recovery, when the Bank of England (BoE) opted for a hawkish hold.

articles.title: Canada: Growth outlook steady with partial rebound – RBC
articles.expand: Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) economists Claire Fan and Abbey Xu expect Canadian GDP to be essentially flat in January after December’s 0.2% gain, with weakness concentrated in autos and housing but offset by energy and retail.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Soft tone but 1.14 base holds – Scotiabank
articles.expand: Scotiabank strategists Shaun Osborne and Eric Theoret report EUR/USD has slipped slightly below yesterday’s low as Dollar strength persists. ECB data show moderating inflation expectations, while President Lagarde signaled caution on rate moves given energy-price risks.

articles.title: Euro area: War-driven energy shock reshapes ECB path – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Dr. Vincent Stamer updates Euro area inflation projections incorporating the Iran War and associated energy shock. The bank expects higher Oil and natural gas prices to push headline inflation above 3% in 2026 before easing back to the ECB’s 2% target by 2027.

articles.title: USD/CAD holds gains above 1.3850 amid fears of a protracted war in Iran
articles.expand: The US Dollar (USD) keeps marching higher against the Canadian Dollar (CAD) on Friday.

articles.title: ECB's Muller: Not sure we need to wait for fully visible second round effects to act
articles.expand: ECB might not need to wait for fully visible second round effects before increasing rates Once energy prices have remained elevated for several weeks, one can already be reasonably confident that second round effects are likely I would not be surprised if by the next policy meeting, elevated energy prices were already showing up more broadly in the...

articles.title: Japanese bond yields surge on inflation concerns, BOJ signals
articles.expand: Japanese government bond yields ​rose across the curve on ​Friday, as recent hawkish central bank signals and the Middle East ​war heightened inflation concerns and prompted investors to reprice the path of rate hikes.

articles.title: How have interest rate expectations changed after this week's events?
articles.expand: Rate hikes by year-end ECB: 85 bps (71% probability of rate hike at the next meeting) BoE: 81 bps (84% probability of rate hike at the next meeting) BoC: 76 bps (77% probability of no change at the next meeting) RBA: 75 bps (72% probability of rate hike at the next meeting) RBNZ: 75 bps (88% probability of no change at the next meeting) BoJ: 54 bps...

articles.title: Fast cash, slow ruin: Examining Ghana’s digital loan trend
articles.expand: A few years ago, the average Ghanaian who needed to borrow money would have to visit a bank, queue behind the counter, and explain why he needed the cash. Now, the man on the street can get a loan on his mobile phone within minutes, and the way he thinks about borrowing money is changing. Digital borrowing is about speed, privacy, and convenience.

articles.title: BOJ would hike rates in April "if you think about it normally", says former governor
articles.expand: Long time no see, Mr. Kuroda. He spokes to the Asahi newspaper in an interview and shared some of his thoughts on BOJ policy setting at the moment. The BOJ would raise the policy rate in April if you think about it normally The US-Iran war would only serve to accelerate interest rate hikes It should not place a pause on the process of monetary poli...

articles.title: Ex-BOJ chief Kuroda sees 3-4 more rate hikes to 1.5% in 2027, Asahi interview shows
articles.expand: Former Bank of Japan ​Governor Haruhiko Kuroda told ‌the Asahi newspaper that he saw "no ​problem" in the ​Japanese central bank raising ⁠the interest rate ​by 0.25% for ​3-4 times from the current 0.75%, to reach "around 1.5%" ​in 2027.

articles.title: Dollar Rides Haven Demand as Middle East Talks Ring Hollow
articles.expand: The dollar hovered near multi-month peaks on Friday as investors sought safety in the shadow of an intensifying Middle East war and mounting doubts over any path to de-escalation. Markets were on edge following another rollercoaster week as US President Donald Trump again extended a deadline for striking Iran's energy facilities into April, even as...

articles.title: Bank of England lowers cost of on-demand liquidity facility
articles.expand: The Bank of England said on Friday it was lowering ​the cost for financial institutions to access its on-demand liquidity support ‌as it shifts away from lenders holding large interest-bearing reserves at the central bank.

articles.title: UK Retail Sales Post First Drop in Three Months, ONS Says
articles.expand: UK retail sales declined for the first time since November, with consumers reining in their spending even before the Iran war clouded Britain’s economic outlook.

articles.title: Global Markets | Australian shares end flat as investors remain cautious on Middle East war
articles.expand: Indian shares saw little movement on Friday. Financial sector losses countered gains in mining stocks. Investors remained cautious. This followed Iran's rejection of a US proposal. Global markets have been unsettled by ongoing conflict. The benchmark S&P/ASX 200 index experienced a slight dip. However, it managed to end a three-week losing streak.

articles.title: BOJ re-estimates Japan's estimated natural rate of interest after review

articles.title: Chicago corn falls, tracking losses in crude oil
articles.expand: BEIJING: Chicago corn futures fell on Friday, tracking a pullback in crude oil prices as investors assessed the market impact of the Middle East conflict. Wheat fell and soybeans stayed unchanged.

articles.title: Stagflation Trap Could Tie RBA’s Hawkish Hand, and Send AUD/JPY to 103
articles.expand: AUD/JPY’s selloff is accelerating as renewed risk aversion combines with a growing “stagflation trap” that is undermining the Reserve Bank of Australia’s hawkish outlook. While markets still price around a 72% chance of a May rate hike, surging energy and fertilizer costs are increasingly seen as a drag on growth rather than a simple inflation [&#8...

articles.title: AUDUSD trades to the downside and tests a key target floor area between 0.6896 and 0.6910
articles.expand: The AUDUSD is typically a classic risk-on/risk-off barometer, but since the start of the Iran/US–Israel conflict on February 28, price action has been anything but one-directional. The pair has traded to new highs for the year, while also rotating back down to test late-January lows — a sign of heightened uncertainty and two-way volatility. Earlier...

articles.expand: The USDCHF found a solid base in yesterday’s trading, leaning against a confluence of key technical levels that helped define both risk and opportunity for traders. articles.expand: The USDCAD continues to push higher, with the pair trading at its strongest level since January 20 as buyers remain firmly in control of the short-term trend. The latest leg higher began late Tuesday from a low at 1.37406, and since then, the price action has followed a textbook bullish progression—breaking key technical levels, consolidating, and ...

articles.title: Precio del Oro XAU/USD: Niveles Críticos Que Podrían Definir la Dirección del Mercado
articles.expand: XAU/USD consolida niveles cercanos a $4.550, con tendencia bajista predominante y posibles repuntes correctivos hacia resistencias técnicas.

articles.title: BoE’s Breeden: ‘Lackluster’ Growth is the UK’s Disinflationary Shield
articles.expand: Bank of England Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden argued today that “second-round effects” from the Iran energy shock are less likely to take root in the UK. Citing rising slack in the labor market and a lackluster outlook for economic activity, Breeden suggested that diminished pricing power for firms and workers acts as a natural buffer [...

articles.title: BoE's Breeden: Current context different from last energy shock in 2022
articles.expand: Firms and workers are likely to have less price and wage bargaining power, so second round effects less likely Not wise to act before we have sufficient information Will know more on balance of risks and scale and duration of shock by April meeting No straight line between energy prices and rates Not surprising that rate expectations have moved BoE...

articles.title: BOJ set to release indicators for estimated core CPI every month now
articles.expand: The BOJ has announced that it will begin publishing data on the estimated core consumer price inflation (CPI) rate, so as to better capture developments and trends in prices. It says that the data will be published under the "Indicators for Core CPI", coming with the following measures: CPI that excludes "institutional factors" Trimmed mean Weighte...
